_description: Rules governing how NES APU register state is converted to MIDI note_on/note_off events in the NSF extraction pipeline. Every note boundary in the output MIDI is determined by these rules.
_authority: nsf_to_reaper.py build_midi() function, synth_fidelity.md section 4, NESMUSICTRANSLATEDTOMIDI.md

period_change_rule.description: A note boundary occurs when the period register changes to a different value with volume > 0. Period stays the same = same note continues. Period changes = old note ends, new note begins.

pulse_channels.condition: midi_note changes (period_to_midi(period) != prev_midi) AND vol > 0 AND period > 8
pulse_channels.on_change_to_new_note: emit note_off for prev_midi, then note_on for new midi_note
pulse_channels.on_change_to_silence: emit note_off for prev_midi (when new midi_note == 0)
pulse_channels.on_change_from_silence: emit note_on for new midi_note (when prev_midi == 0)

triangle_channel.condition: midi_note changes (period_to_midi(period, is_tri=True) != prev_midi) AND linear > 0 AND period > 2
triangle_channel.gate_signal: linear counter ($4008 bits 0-6) must be > 0 for note to be active

volume_gate_rule.description: Volume affects whether a note is considered active but does NOT directly cause note_off. A note with vol=0 produces midi_note=0 which triggers note_off through the period_change_rule path.
volume_gate_rule.pulse_logic: midi_note = period_to_midi(period) if period > 8 and vol > 0 else 0
volume_gate_rule.triangle_logic: midi_note = period_to_midi(period, is_tri=True) if period > 2 and linear > 0 else 0
volume_gate_rule.important: Duration is determined by period changes, not volume reaching zero. A note can be silent for its last N frames if CC11 decays to 0 before the period changes. This is correct NES behavior.

triangle_specific.gate_behavior: linear > 0 means triangle is sounding; linear == 0 means silence
triangle_specific.cc11_always_127: Triangle CC11 is always emitted as 127 (gate signal only, no hardware volume)
triangle_specific.octave_offset: Triangle sounds 1 octave lower than pulse for same period value due to 32-step sequencer vs 16-step

noise_specific.detection_method: volume gate transition (vol > 0 after prev_vol <= 0 triggers drum hit)
noise_specific.no_period_change_detection: Noise does NOT use period-change-based note boundaries. It uses volume transitions.

timing.quantization_note: 1 frame = 16 ticks at 128.6 BPM. No rounding, no quantization. Timing resolution exactly matches NES frame rate.

cc12_duty.description: 12.5%, 25%, 50%, 75% duty cycles mapped to CC12 ranges

known_risks.description: Games implementing vibrato via rapid small period changes produce a stream of very short notes (48 ticks / 3 frames) instead of a single note with pitch bend. Sounds choppy on synths with attack time.
known_risks.affected_games: Games using software vibrato (not hardware sweep unit)
known_risks.possible_fix: Detect rapid period oscillation (+/- 1-2 semitones within 4 frames) and convert to MIDI pitch bend

known_risks.description: The emulator captures APU state once per frame (after PLAY() returns). If the driver writes a register twice in one frame, only the final value is seen. Can lose very fast trills or mid-frame effects.
known_risks.severity: negligible for all 34 games in library

known_risks.description: Only 3 GM drum buckets (kick/snare/hi-hat) for 16 possible noise periods. Real NES games use all 16 periods with different volume envelopes for a wider range of percussion timbres.
known_risks.possible_fix: Map all 16 noise periods to distinct MIDI notes and build a more nuanced drum envelope table in the synth

known_risks.description: Smooth pitch slides become a series of discrete chromatic note events, one per frame.
NES hardware sweep unit produces continuous pitch changes. These are captured as discrete period changes per frame, not as MIDI pitch bend events.
DPCM (channel 5) samples for voice clips, bass drums, or samples are not extracted.
VRC6, FDS, MMC5, N163, Sunsoft 5B expansion audio channels are not handled. Only standard 2A03 channels.
